messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

This patch only contains changes that I don't know how to test or build. Skipping

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

This looks like it has the drawback that it doesn't create the "mount.lustre_tgt" symlink inside the build tree, only when doing "make install" and "make rpms"?  So it might make Janitor (and developers) unhappy if we ever require this filesystem to exist (instead of falling back to "mount.lustre" internally.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> This looks like it has the drawback that it doesn't create the "mount.lustre_tgt" symlink inside the build tree, only when doing "make install" and "make rpms"?  So it might make Janitor (and developers) unhappy if we ever require this filesystem to exist (instead of falling back to "mount.lustre" internally.

Yeah, I thought about this.  I'm not sure how to handle that - it's a build product...  I suppose I have to create and package the symlink file, right?

I'll see about doing that - I see your point, it was already bugging me.

Hm.  Looking at this again and doing some reading, I think creating the symlink is usually done during the install(?).

I realize now I don't really understand your concern - can you say more?  Creating the symlink during the make rather than the install is a bit of a pain in terms of getting the path right, I think...

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

I guess we can defer the issue until such a time that using mount.lustre_tgt is required by some code, and not an optional add-on (originally done to allow the mount code to be cleaned up between client and server).  This is fixing a problem today, so it should take precedence.

parents.subject: LU-18792 tests: fix sanity-hsm test_26e for interop testing

commit.subject: LU-17843 build: mount.lustre_tgt as symlink
commit.message:
LU-17843 build: mount.lustre_tgt as symlink

mount.lustre_tgt was always intended to be a symlink to the
main mount.lustre, but instead it's an identical build item.

This results in this rpmbuild warning (an error on some systems):
    Duplicate build-ids .../mount.lustre and .../mount.lustre_tgt

This patch resolves this by making it a proper symlink as was
originally intended.

Test-Parameters: trivial
Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <patrick.farrell@oracle.com>
Change-Id: Id5f90df94fcc5c73a93fd6c1311b09af429c1440
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55079
